blocksCertification: Low-product-density how-to: only 4 genuinely relevant verified products (Honeycomb Alpha, generic desk clamp, Monstertech table mount, Trak Racer TR80), reused across a 4-image shotgrid + 3 gearpicks = 7 embedded verified images. Reaching the 8-verified-image bar would require forcing an irrelevant product card, which the run rules forbid — so candidacy is intentionally left false.

Two products central to the article (powered USB hub, MOZA AY210 FFB yoke) have NO verified product image and are text+Amazon-only. Neither generates a /…/gear/ detail page until its image is verified, so they cannot become gearpicks/body visuals yet.

heroImage is a stand-in (Honeycomb Alpha yoke) — the only verified image that fits a mounting article. A purpose-made editorial mounting hero should be generated by Codex and added to verified-product-images.json before certification.
Prices left as 'check current' (Monstertech ~$100 each, Trak Racer TR80 ~$499, DIY 8020 ~$325+ parts, MOZA AY210 ~$699/$848) — confirm current US figures before removing the flags.
notes: Full gold-structure rewrite: verdict-first opening, four-rung mounting ladder (box clamps -> aluminum desk mount -> powered USB hub -> cockpit frame), a comparison table, tip + forum callouts (original paraphrase), setup-tax warnings, who-should-skip, buy-order recap, and a dated Sources Checked shelf with real links. Author = curator (Mac Donovan). goldStatus left 'refresh' per run rules. ALL 7 embedded images are verified product photos (confirmed against verified-product-images.json); the two previously-embedded UNVERIFIED images (moza-ay210-ffb-yoke.jpg, desktop-pilot-10-port-usb-hub.jpg) were removed and demoted to text+Amazon links. Exactly 2 frontmatter fences. `npx astro build` compiles clean (166 pages). Not a certification candidate until a real mounting hero + verified USB-hub/MOZA photos land and prices are confirmed; the biggest content lift for Codex is the two flagged infographics (mounting ladder + USB-power decision tree), which suit this how-to better than more product cards.
